title: dactylorhiza fuchsii var. okellyi
intro: Dactylorhiza fuchsii var. okellyi, also known as Okelly's spotted orchid, is a subspecies of the spotted orchid plant, a terrestrial orchid native to Ireland and parts of the UK. Traditionally, the Okelly's spotted orchid has been used in herbal medicine due to its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the Okelly's spotted orchid is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential allergic reactions and interactions with other medications.
